items.text: Private Equity, Hedge Funds and Venture Capital Law
Bank Secrecy and Anti-Money Laundering Law

children.label: Private Equity, Hedge Funds and Venture Capital Law
Bank Secrecy and Anti-Money Laundering Law

candidates.label: Commercial Finance Law
candidates.definition: Law supporting the function of providing loans or other financing to businesses.

candidates.label: Asset-Based Lending Practice
candidates.definition: Asset-Based Lending Practice involves legal work related to providing loans secured by a borrower's assets, such as inventory, accounts receivable, or equipment. This practice includes drafting and negotiating loan agreements, conducting due diligence on collateral, and ensuring compliance with relevant laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Lending Practice
candidates.definition: A transaction whereby property is lent or given to another on condition of return or, where the loan is of money, repayment. In a loan of money, the money lent becomes the property of the borrower during the period of the loan against an undertaking to return a sum of an equivalent amount either on demand or on a specified date or in accordance with an agreed schedule of repayments.

candidates.label: Financing Practice
candidates.definition: Agreements to finance a business or other entity.

candidates.label: M&A E Financing Practice
candidates.definition: M&A E Financing Practice refers to the legal practice area that deals with mergers and acquisitions as well as financing transactions related to those deals.
synonyms: M&A Eファイナンス実践

candidates.label: Broker-Dealer
candidates.definition: A Broker-Dealer is a financial firm or individual that buys and sells securities on behalf of clients (acting as a broker) or for its own account (acting as a dealer). Broker-dealers are essential in facilitating securities transactions, providing market liquidity, and offering investment advice and services to individual and institutional investors.

candidates.label: Broker
candidates.definition: A broker is an individual or firm employed by others to plan and organize sales or negotiate contracts for a commission.

candidates.label: Dealer
candidates.definition: A dealer is a person, company, or entity that buys and sells securities for his or her or its own account and not for others.

candidates.label: Broker-Dealer Law
candidates.definition: Law surrounding individuals engaged in effecting transactions in securities for others or for their own account.
synonyms: Droit des courtiers et des concessionnaires
ブローカー・ディーラー法

candidates.label: Derivatives and Futures Law
candidates.definition: Law governing derivatives.
synonyms: Droit des dérivés et des contrats à terme
デリバティブおよび先物取引法

candidates.label: Investment Funds
candidates.definition: Investment Funds are pooled investment vehicles that collect capital from multiple investors to invest in a diversified portfolio of assets. These funds include various types such as mutual funds, hedge funds, and real estate investment trusts (REITs), each with distinct investment strategies and regulatory structures.

candidates.label: Investment Companies Law
candidates.definition: Regulations on companies that engage primarily in investing, reinvesting, and trading in securities, and whose own securities are offered to the investing public.

items.item_text: Private Equity, Hedge Funds and Venture Capital Law

candidates.label: Private Equity, Hedge Funds and Venture Capital Law
candidates.definition: Private Equity, Hedge Funds, and Venture Capital Law encompasses the legal frameworks governing private equity investments, hedge funds, and venture capital financing. It involves regulations related to fund formation, management, investment, compliance, investor rights, and financial transactions within these sectors.
synonyms: Droit des fonds d'investissement, des fonds spéculatifs et du capital-risque
Gesetzgebung für Private Equity, Hedgefonds und Risikokapital
Lei de Private Equity, Hedge Funds e Venture Capital
Ley de Capital Privado, Fondos de Cobertura y Capital de Riesgo
Private Equity, Hedge Funds and Venture Capital Law
חוק הון פרטי, קרנות השתלמות והון סיכון
प्राइवेट इक्विटी, हेज फंड और वेंचर कैपिटल कानून
プライベートエクイティ、ヘッジファンド、ベンチャーキャピタル法
私募股权、对冲基金和风险投资法律

hierarchy_path.label: Private Equity, Hedge Funds and Venture Capital Law

candidates.label: Structured Finance Law
candidates.definition: Law surrounding securitization and asset-backed securities.
synonyms: ストラクチャードファイナンス法

candidates.label: Structured Finance Agreement
candidates.definition: A Structured Finance Agreement pertains to complex financial transactions that pool and repackage financial assets, often for the purpose of risk diversification and enhanced returns. These agreements govern the terms, parties involved, and the structure of such transactions.

candidates.label: Structured Finance Law
candidates.definition: Law surrounding securitization and asset-backed securities.
synonyms: ストラクチャードファイナンス法

candidates.label: Collateralized Loan Obligation
candidates.definition: A CLO is a type of structured finance instrument backed by a pool of loans. These loans are bundled together and then sliced into tranches with varying degrees of risk and reward, which are then sold to investors.

candidates.label: Collateral
candidates.definition: Collateral is an asset or property pledged by a borrower to a lender as security for a loan or other obligation, to mitigate the risk of default. If the borrower fails to meet the obligations, the lender has the right to seize the collateral.

candidates.label: Obligation
candidates.definition: An obligation is a legal or moral duty that requires a certain course of action or behavior, often enforceable by law or ethical principles.

candidates.label: Regulations
candidates.definition: Rules or orders issued by a governmental regulatory agency and having the force of law.

candidates.label: Compliance
candidates.definition: Deontic compliance refers to the conformity in action, behavior, or attitude to a set of rules or standards, as prescribed by laws, regulations, or ethical principles.

items.item_text: Bank Secrecy and Anti-Money Laundering Law

candidates.label: Bank Secrecy and Anti-Money Laundering Law
candidates.definition: Law regulating financial institutions relating to money laundering.
synonyms: Bank Secrecy and Anti-Money Laundering Law
Lei de Sigilo Bancário e de Combate à Lavagem de Dinheiro
Ley de Secreto Bancario y Anti-Lavado de Dinero
Loi sur le secret bancaire et la lutte contre le blanchiment d'argent
חוק הסוד הבנקאי ומניעת כביסת כספים
बैंक गोपनीयता और धन प्रवर्तन रोकथाम कानून
銀行秘密保持およびマネーロンダリング防止法

hierarchy_path.label: Bank Secrecy and Anti-Money Laundering Law

candidates.label: Cryptocurrency Law
candidates.definition: Cryptocurrency law encompasses the legal rules and regulations governing the use, creation, and exchange of digital currencies, such as Bitcoin and Ethereum. It addresses issues related to security, taxation, and compliance with financial regulations.

candidates.label: Cryptocurrency
candidates.definition: A cryptocurrency is a digital or virtual currency that is secured by cryptography, which makes it nearly impossible to counterfeit or double-spend.

candidates.label: Currency
candidates.definition: A standardization of money that is used, circulated, or exchanged (e.g., banknotes, coins).

candidates.label: Regulator
candidates.definition: A regulator is an entity, typically a government agency or authority, responsible for overseeing and enforcing specific regulations within a particular industry or sector. Regulators ensure compliance with laws, standards, and guidelines to protect public interests, such as consumer safety, environmental protection, and market fairness.

candidates.label: Regulations
candidates.definition: Rules or orders issued by a governmental regulatory agency and having the force of law.

candidates.label: Compliance
candidates.definition: Deontic compliance refers to the conformity in action, behavior, or attitude to a set of rules or standards, as prescribed by laws, regulations, or ethical principles.

candidates.label: Regulatory Enforcement
candidates.definition: The process of enforcement by a regulatory agency through an administrative procedure outside of any court system.

candidates.label: Regulatory Compliance
candidates.definition: The process by which an entity (i.e., organization or person) ensures that it observes and complies with the external statutory laws and regulations.
synonyms: 規制コンプライアンス

candidates.label: Insurance Regulatory Practice
candidates.definition: Insurance Regulatory Practice involves representing companies, brokers, and other entities in complying with the regulatory requirements imposed by insurance regulatory authorities. This legal service includes obtaining licenses, navigating regulatory changes, and ensuring adherence to financial solvency, consumer protection, and reporting obligations.
